title: daphne odora f. leucantha
intro: Daphne odora f. leucantha, also known as the white-wintered or white-flowered daphne, is a fragrant and evergreen shrub native to Asia, belonging to the family Thymelaeaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Daphne odora f. leucantha include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds. Daphne odora f. leucantha can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent, such as ethanol.
